skill.name: minimax-docx
skill.description: Create, edit, format, validate, preview, and template Word DOCX documents with an OpenXML SDK based .NET toolkit. The skill supports new document creation, content replacement, table filling, template style transfer, validation gates, and document preview workflows.
skill.summary: Build and modify professional Word DOCX files with OpenXML tools.
skill.author: MiniMax-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and critical patterns, but most reviewed instances are OpenXML terminology, XML namespaces, documentation examples, or legitimate installer and CLI behavior. No prompt-injection attempt or confirmed malicious exfiltration was found. The skill still carries medium risk because it includes executable setup scripts, remote dependency installation, package restore, filesystem writes, and template relationship copying.

medium_findings.title: Privileged Dependency Installation and Remote Script Download
medium_findings.description: The setup workflow can run sudo package-manager commands, download the Microsoft dotnet install script into /tmp, modify the user PATH through .bashrc, restore NuGet packages, and build the .NET project. This is legitimate setup behavior for the skill, but it executes external code and changes the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly visible and their purpose is dependency installation. I found no malicious target, but the elevated execution and remote installer behavior are real operational risk.

medium_findings.title: DOCX Template Relationships Are Copied Into Output
medium_findings.description: The template application code copies external relationships from template parts into the output document. This can preserve external hyperlinks or remote resources from an untrusted template, which may trigger tracking or network access when the resulting DOCX is opened.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code explicitly iterates ExternalRelationships and adds them to the target part. This is useful for template fidelity, but risky when template files are not trusted.

medium_findings.title: Environment Checks and Preview Helpers Execute Local Toolchain Commands
medium_findings.description: The helper scripts run local commands such as dotnet, pandoc, soffice, unzip, grep, chmod, and package-manager recommendations. Inputs are generally quoted and the behavior matches the skill purpose, but users should treat these scripts as executable tooling rather than passive documentation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are observable and aligned with environment validation and DOCX preview. The risk is moderate because execution is local and expected, not hidden.

low_findings.title: OpenXML Namespace URLs Are Static Identifiers
low_findings.description: Many hardcoded URL findings are XML namespace constants or schema URIs used by OpenXML and XSD files. They identify XML vocabularies and do not imply outbound network requests by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed paths contain OpenXML namespace declarations and relationship constants. These are required identifiers, not active network operations.

low_findings.title: Documentation and Sample Keywords Triggered Security Heuristics
low_findings.description: The critical and high heuristic findings for weak cryptography, C2 terms, SAM text, Unicode escapes, and bracket chains largely appear inside OpenXML guides and sample code comments about document structures, symbols, and formatting examples. No evidence found that these samples implement credential access, C2 behavior, or obfuscated execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review showed formatting samples and reference material, not credential harvesting or command-and-control logic. Some documentation is large, so this remains a sampled contextual dismissal.

low_findings.title: Shell Command Substitution Is Used for Local Path and Metadata Handling
low_findings.description: Backticks and command substitution findings in scripts and guides generally represent documented CLI examples or local shell operations such as basename, dirname, du, wc, and version checks. The reviewed script inputs are quoted and no direct shell injection vector was confirmed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed command substitutions are conventional shell scripting patterns. A full command-injection proof would require deeper testing, but no unsafe concatenation was observed in sampled files.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Installer Download Followed by Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The Unix setup script downloads dotnet-install.sh from dot.net to /tmp, marks it executable, and runs it. HTTPS and a reputable source reduce risk, but the pattern should be reviewed before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The download, chmod, and execution sequence is explicit. The target is legitimate, but the execution pattern is inherently sensitive.

dangerous_patterns.title: External DOCX Relationship Preservation
dangerous_patterns.description: External relationships from templates are copied into output parts. This can preserve remote links from source templates and should be limited to trusted template files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code path is clear and directly copies external relationship URIs. The risk depends on whether the template is trusted.

content.value_statement: Word document automation is fragile when structure, styles, and validation are handled manually. This skill provides OpenXML-based workflows for creating, editing, templating, and checking DOCX files.
actual_capabilities: Creates new DOCX reports, letters, memos, and academic documents from structured content.
Edits existing DOCX files with text replacement, placeholder filling, table updates, and section insertion.
Applies styles, themes, numbering, headers, footers, and section properties from template DOCX files.
Runs validation checks against OpenXML structure rules and bundled business-rule XSD files.
Previews DOCX content as plain text through pandoc or a raw XML fallback.
Provides many OpenXML C# samples for styles, tables, images, fields, comments, and track changes.
limitations: Requires a local .NET SDK and may need optional tools such as pandoc or LibreOffice.
Setup scripts can install dependencies and should be reviewed before running on managed systems.
Complex template merges still require careful review of headers, footers, section breaks, and numbering.
It does not guarantee visual parity across every Word version, font set, or operating system.

use_cases.title: Generate Business Reports
use_cases.description: Create polished reports, memos, and proposals with consistent page setup, headings, tables, and validation checks.

use_cases.title: Fill Existing Forms
use_cases.description: Replace placeholders, update text, and fill tables in existing DOCX files while preserving most original formatting.

use_cases.title: Apply Institutional Templates
use_cases.description: Transfer styles, headers, footers, numbering, and section properties from approved DOCX templates to working drafts.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a DOCX report titled [title] for [audience]. Include an executive summary, three sections, a conclusion, page numbers, and a clean professional style.

prompt_templates.title: Fill a Template Form
prompt_templates.prompt: Use [input.docx] as the source file. Fill the placeholders with the provided values, preserve formatting, preview the result, and save the completed file as [output.docx].

prompt_templates.title: Reformat to Match Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the formatting from [template.docx] to [source.docx]. Preserve the source content, transfer styles and section properties, validate the result, and report any changes.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Complex Technical Document
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a multi-section DOCX with custom headings, tables, figures, headers, footers, a table of contents, and validation checks. Use the relevant OpenXML samples before writing C#.

output_examples.input: A request to draft a quarterly operating report with tables, headers, and page numbers.
output_examples.output: A generated DOCX report with structured sections, professional styling, numbered pages, and a validation summary.

output_examples.input: A source DOCX and an institutional template that must define the final formatting.
output_examples.output: A reformatted DOCX that keeps the source content while applying template styles, section settings, and header or footer rules.

output_examples.input: A DOCX form containing placeholders and a list of replacement values.
output_examples.output: A completed DOCX form plus a preview of the filled content for quick verification.

best_practices: Review setup scripts before running them, especially on systems with restricted package policies.
Preview and validate every generated DOCX before delivery.
Use trusted templates when applying headers, footers, relationships, and section properties.
anti_patterns: Do not run dependency installers with elevated privileges without reviewing the commands first.
Do not apply formatting from unknown DOCX templates without checking external links and relationships.
Do not skip validation after structural edits, complex tables, section breaks, or track changes.

faq.question: Does this skill create real DOCX files?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses a .NET OpenXML toolkit to create and modify Word DOCX packages.

faq.question: Can it edit an existing Word document?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports text replacement, placeholder filling, table updates, section insertion, and template formatting workflows.

faq.question: Does it need external tools?
faq.answer: The core toolkit needs .NET. Optional preview and legacy DOC conversion use pandoc and LibreOffice.

faq.question: Is it safe to use with untrusted templates?
faq.answer: Use caution. Templates can contain external links or relationships that may be copied into the output document.

faq.question: Can it handle Chinese or CJK document formatting?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes CJK typography references and samples, but local fonts affect rendering quality.

faq.question: Does it replace Microsoft Word?
faq.answer: No. It automates DOCX generation and editing, but final visual review in Word or a compatible viewer is still recommended.
